title: BlackHoleResearch: A Multiwavelength FITS Explorer for Black Hole Astrophysics
description:
BlackHoleResearch is an open-source, Streamlit-based multiwavelength FITS explorer for black hole astrophysics research and teaching. It loads NASA-archive FITS data from Chandra, XMM-Newton, NuSTAR, ROSAT, JWST (NIRSpec/NIRCam), WISE, 2MASS, VLA, and DSS; renders images with proper WCS overlays and asinh/log/zscale stretches; bins X-ray event lists; fits descriptive power laws to OGIP PHA spectra; computes Lomb-Scargle periodograms and Vaughan+2003 fractional rms variability; and assembles multi-band Spectral Energy Distributions for classical AGN regimes (Seyfert 2 NGC 1068, low-luminosity AGN M87, stellar-mass BH X-ray binary Cyg X-1).

Phase 2 adds JWST Little Red Dot (LRD) diagnostics: L_X/L_UV vs Lusso & Risaliti 2016, Balmer-break detector, and broad-Hα-based black-hole-mass estimation (Reines & Volonteri 2015).

Built on astropy, astroquery, photutils, specutils. MIT-licensed. Includes a documented FITS-handling pitfalls catalog covering event-list-vs-image confusion, WCS gotchas, GTI artifacts, OGIP RMF/ARF requirements, Vega-vs-AB photometry, mission time systems, and memmap invalidation.
references: Shakura, N. I. & Sunyaev, R. A. (1973). Black holes in binary systems. Observational appearance. A&A 24, 337.
Vaughan, S., Edelson, R., Warwick, R. S., Uttley, P. (2003). MNRAS 345, 1271.
Stern, D. et al. (2012). Mid-infrared selection of AGN with WISE. ApJ 753, 30.
Donley, J. L. et al. (2012). Revised IRAC AGN wedge. ApJ 748, 142.
Lusso, E. & Risaliti, G. (2016). The L_X / L_UV relation. ApJ 819, 154.
Greene, J. E. et al. (2024). UNCOVER spectroscopy of LRDs. ApJ 964, 39.
Maiolino, R. et al. (2024). JADES infant black holes. A&A 691, A145.
